Live example · video 1

The homography court grid, actually running

Full re-run of Badminton_video_example.mp4 through this repo's real pipeline with the proposed method turned on, plus a fix so the diagnostic overlay actually draws it (it previously always drew the old rectangle grid regardless).

What changed to make this possible: two small code changes, no new machinery.
  1. src/pipeline/debug_overlay.py — the grid-drawing function only ever drew the flat pixel-space rectangle. It now draws the real perspective-correct trapezoid (inverse-projected from the homography) whenever one is available, and only falls back to the rectangle otherwise.
  2. src/main.py — added an opt-in --homography flag wired to the existing calibrate_from_video(..., use_homography=...) parameter (previously hardcoded off).

Also fixed a real bug this surfaced: recalibrate_from_shuttle_positions() (the second pass that tightens row bounds using real shuttle data) was unconditionally clearing the homography field. With it enabled, every shot's zone would have silently reverted to the plain proportional grid right after calibration — before a single shot was scored. It now preserves homography and only refines the proportional fallback's own bounds.

Update (docs/PRD_v2.6.md, Phase G) — two follow-up refinements from reviewing this exact demo:
  1. Real BWF service-line row bands, not equal thirds. The front/mid boundary now sits at the short service line (198cm from net) and the mid/back boundary at the long service line (76cm from the baseline) — not at arbitrary 1/3 and 2/3 points. Mid (4/5/6) is now the largest band by far (59% of half-court depth); front (7/8/9) and back (1/2/3) are much thinner bands hugging the net and baseline respectively. Applied only to the homography path — see PRD Phase G.2 for why the proportional fallback keeps equal thirds.
  2. White-line corner refinement (court_detector.refine_corners_with_lines) — the green-surface-derived corners used to run a little short of the true painted boundary on some edges (confirmed by zooming into corner regions of these exact frames). The refinement now snaps each edge to detected white-line segments, re-intersecting for boundary-accurate corners, with a conservative fallback if too few lines are found. The frames below already have this applied — the grid now hugs the true green/blue mat edge instead of sitting visibly inside it.

Command used: python -m src.main ground_truth_template/Badminton_video_example.mp4 --homography --debug-frames 10 --debug-video

homography 9-zone grid (bird's-eye, perspective-correct)
player foot position
racket (best-effort)
shuttlecock (TrackNetV3)

Generated by re-running this repo's own pipeline (src/main.py) end-to-end on ground_truth_template/Badminton_video_example.mp4, with --homography enabled, the debug-overlay fix, real BWF service-line row bands, and white-line corner refinement all applied (docs/PRD_v2.6.md, Phase G). Not yet committed — these are working-tree changes pending your review.
